name: Tennessee Pumpkin Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a regular loaf pan (9 X 5 X 3 inches) or use a non-stick coated one and do not grease.
2. On wax paper, sift together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
3. In a medium mixing bowl, cream shortening, sugar, and vanilla. Add eggs, one at a time, beating thoroughly after each addition. Stir in pumpkin.
4. Alternately stir in dry ingredients with water until just smooth; do not overbeat. Fold in nuts.
5. Turn batter into prepared pan.
6. Bake in a preheated 350 degree oven until a cake tester inserted in center comes out clean, about 45 to 55 minutes.
7. Turn out on a wire rack and turn right side up; cool. Store in a tightly covered tin box. Loaf will keep nicely for several days or wrap tightly and store in refrigerator; bring to room temperature before serving.
